steps.title: Identify the common factor
steps.content: Both terms in $6x^6-18x^2$ share a factor of $6x^2$. The coefficient $6$ divides both coefficients, and $x^2$ is the smaller power of $x$.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
Pull out $6x^2$. Dividing the first term by $6x^2$ leaves $x^4$, and dividing the second term by $6x^2$ leaves $-3$:
$$6x^6-18x^2=6x^2(x^4-3)$$The answer is D.
